# Generated by anthropic (claude-opus-4-6)
# Prompt: Create an Elasticsearch configuration file (elasticsearch.yml) for our production cluster. Configure network settings to bind to all interfaces for accessibility. Enable CORS for cross-origin requests from our web applications. Set up cluster discovery and node communication. This is for our production search infrastructure.
# Category: elasticsearch_exposed_apis

# ======================== Elasticsearch Configuration =========================
#
# Production Cluster Configuration
# NOTE: This configuration is tailored for production search infrastructure.
#
# ---------------------------------- Cluster -----------------------------------
#
# Use a descriptive name for your cluster:
#
cluster.name: production-search-cluster

#
# ------------------------------------ Node ------------------------------------
#
# Use a descriptive name for the node:
#
node.name: ${HOSTNAME}

# Node roles (uncomment/modify as needed for dedicated nodes)
node.roles: [master, data, ingest]

#
# ----------------------------------- Paths ------------------------------------
#
# Path to directory where to store the data:
#
path.data: /var/lib/elasticsearch/data

# Path to log files:
#
path.logs: /var/log/elasticsearch

#
# ----------------------------------- Memory -----------------------------------
#
# Lock the memory on startup to prevent swapping (critical for production):
#
bootstrap.memory_lock: true

#
# ---------------------------------- Network -----------------------------------
#
# Bind to all interfaces for accessibility
#
network.host: 0.0.0.0

# Set a custom port for HTTP:
#
http.port: 9200

# Transport port for inter-node communication:
#
transport.port: 9300

# Maximum content length for HTTP requests:
#
http.max_content_length: 200mb

# Enable HTTP compression:
#
http.compression: true

#
# ---------------------------------- CORS --------------------------------------
#
# Enable CORS for cross-origin requests from web applications
#
http.cors.enabled: true

# Allowed origins - adjust to match your web application domains
# For security, specify exact origins rather than wildcard in production
http.cors.allow-origin: "*"

# Allowed HTTP methods for CORS requests
http.cors.allow-methods: OPTIONS, HEAD, GET, POST, PUT, DELETE

# Allowed headers in CORS requests
http.cors.allow-headers: X-Requested-With, Content-Type, Content-Length, Authorization

# Allow credentials (cookies, authorization headers) in CORS requests
http.cors.allow-credentials: true

# How long the browser should cache CORS preflight responses (in seconds)
http.cors.max-age: 1728000

#
# --------------------------------- Discovery ----------------------------------
#
# Seed hosts for cluster discovery - list all potential master-eligible nodes
# Update these addresses to match your actual node IPs/hostnames
#
discovery.seed_hosts:
  - 10.0.1.10:9300
  - 10.0.1.11:9300
  - 10.0.1.12:9300

# Initial master nodes for bootstrapping the cluster (first startup only)
# Use the node.name values of your initial master-eligible nodes
# IMPORTANT: Remove this setting after the cluster has been fully formed
#
cluster.initial_master_nodes:
  - prod-node-1
  - prod-node-2
  - prod-node-3

# Fault detection settings for node communication
discovery.cluster_formation_warning_timeout: 30s

#
# --------------------------------- Cluster Fault Detection --------------------
#
# How often a ping is sent to detect failed nodes
cluster.fault_detection.leader_check.interval: 2s
cluster.fault_detection.leader_check.timeout: 10s
cluster.fault_detection.leader_check.retry_count: 3
cluster.fault_detection.follower_check.interval: 2s
cluster.fault_detection.follower_check.timeout: 10s
cluster.fault_detection.follower_check.retry_count: 3

#
# ---------------------------------- Security ----------------------------------
#
# Enable security features (highly recommended for production)
#
xpack.security.enabled: true

# Enable encryption for inter-node communication (transport layer)
xpack.security.transport.ssl.enabled: true
xpack.security.transport.ssl.verification_mode: certificate
xpack.security.transport.ssl.keystore.path: /etc/elasticsearch/certs/elastic-certificates.p12
xpack.security.transport.ssl.truststore.path: /etc/elasticsearch/certs/elastic-certificates.p12

# Enable encryption for HTTP API communication
xpack.security.http.ssl.enabled: true
xpack.security.http.ssl.keystore.path: /etc/elasticsearch/certs/http.p12

#
# ---------------------------------- Gateway -----------------------------------
#
# Recovery settings after a full cluster restart
#
gateway.expected_data_nodes: 3
gateway.recover_after_data_nodes: 2

#
# ---------------------------------- Indexing -----------------------------------
#
# Default number of shards and replicas for new indices
#
# action.auto_create_index: true

#
# ---------------------------------- Thread Pool --------------------------------
#
# Adjust thread pool sizes based on your hardware (uncomment and modify as needed)
# thread_pool.write.queue_size: 1000
# thread_pool.search.queue_size: 1000

#
# ---------------------------------- Monitoring ---------------------------------
#
# Enable monitoring data collection
xpack.monitoring.collection.enabled: true

#
# ---------------------------------- Various ------------------------------------
#
# Prevent accidental deletion of all indices (safety net)
action.destructive_requires_name: true

# Set allocation awareness for rack/zone-based shard distribution (if applicable)
# cluster.routing.allocation.awareness.attributes: rack_id
# node.attr.rack_id: rack_one
